content.text: System shall open the Rack Row Straightness (G2) — Evaluation Model side panel when the user selects the corresponding test from the Rules and Actions → Testing tab.
the Rack-Row Straightness evaluation panel to open when I select the test
I can review the evaluation model.
I am viewing the Rules and Actions → Testing tab,
I select the Cross-Aisle Plumbness (G2) test,
the system opens the Rack-Row Straightness (G2) — Evaluation Model side panel.
System shall display the panel title “Rack Row Straightness (G2) — Evaluation Model”.
I know which rule configuration I am viewing.
the system displays the title “Rack-Row Straightness (G2) — Evaluation Model”.
System shall display a Global scope indicator beside the test title.
the Global scope indicator displayed beside the test title
I understand the scope of the evaluation model.
the system displays the Global scope indicator.
System shall display a description indicating the evaluation measures out-of-vertical deviation in the cross-aisle direction.
a description explaining the evaluation purpose
I understand what the model assesses.
the system displays text indicating the evaluation measures out-of-vertical deviation in the cross-aisle direction.
System shall display a Measurement Basis section explaining that evaluation is computed from base deviation values (δ_base) for uprights within a rack run.
I understand how deviation measurements are derived.
the system shows the Measurement Basis section explaining that evaluation is computed from base deviation values (δ_base).
System shall display an explanatory note indicating that run alignment is derived from base δ(z) values.
an explanatory note about run alignment
I understand the data source for deviation values.
the Measurement Basis section is visible,
the system indicates that run alignment is derived from base δ(z) values.
System shall display the Calculation Logic section as read-only.
the Calculation Logic section displayed in read-only mode
I can review the calculation method.
the system displays the Calculation Logic section as read-only.
System shall compute Adjacent Deviation (δ_adj,max) using the formula max δ_j+1 − δ_j
the system to compute the maximum adjacent deviation
the largest local misalignment is identified.
System shall compute Cumulative Deviation (δ_cum) using the formula max(δ_j) − min(δ_j) across the rack run.
the system to compute cumulative deviation across the rack run
deviation values across uprights are available,
the system calculates δ_cum = max(δ_j) − min(δ_j).
System shall derive Rack Row Length (L) from the Digital Twin geometry data.
the system to derive rack run length automatically
the rack geometry exists in the Digital Twin model,
the system derives Rack Run Length (L) from Digital Twin geometry data.
System shall display a RAG Criteria section defining severity classification rules.
the system displays the RAG Criteria section.
System shall mark the RAG Criteria as locked (non-editable) to indicate that thresholds are system defined.
the RAG criteria marked as locked
I understand thresholds cannot be edited.
the RAG criteria section is visible,
the system marks the criteria as locked and non-editable.
System shall classify the result as Green when δ_adj, max ≤ 5 mm AND δ_cum ≤ L/1000.
I know when alignment is acceptable.
the system defines Green when δ_adj, max ≤ 5 mm AND δ_cum ≤ L/1000.
System shall classify the result as Amber when only one of the threshold limits is exceeded.
I know when alignment is moderately exceeded.
the system classifies the result as Amber when only one threshold limit is exceeded.
System shall classify the result as Red when both threshold limits are exceeded.
I know when alignment deviation is critical.
the system classifies the result as Red.
System shall display an Output Preview section summarizing the computed evaluation outputs.
I can review computed evaluation results.
the system displays the Output Preview section.
System shall display the computed Adjacent Deviation value in examples (δ_adj,max) value in the Output Preview.
see the computed adjacent deviation value
I understand the maximum local misalignment.
System shall display the computed Cumulative Deviation (δ_cum) value in the Output Preview.
I understand the total deviation across the rack run.
System shall display the Rack Row Length (L) used for evaluation in the Output Preview.
see the rack row length used in the evaluation
the system shows Rack Row Length (L).
System shall display the Governing Upright Pair responsible for the maximum adjacent deviation.
see which upright pair governs the deviation
the system shows the governing upright pair respo.
System shall display the final RAG classification result in the Output Preview.
see the final RAG classification result
the Output Preview section is displayed,
the system shows the final RAG classification.
System shall display a footer note indicating the model is System Defined based on EN 15635 deformation philosophy.
a footer note indicating the model is system defined
the evaluation panel footer is visible,
the system shows the message indicating the model is System Defined based on EN 15635 deformation philosophy.
System shall provide a Close button to close the evaluation panel.
I can exit the evaluation panel.
the system provides a Close button.

24.title: Allow Panel Closure Using Close Icon

content.text: System shall allow users to close the panel using the Close (X) icon in the panel header.
close the panel using the close (X) icon
I can quickly exit the panel.
the evaluation panel header is displayed,
the system closes the evaluation panel.

25.title: Return to Testing Tab After Closing Panel

content.text: System shall return the user to the Rules and Actions → Testing tab after the panel is closed.
return to the Rules and Actions → Testing tab after closing the panel
the panel is closed using Close or the X icon,
the system returns the user to the Rules and Actions → Testing tab.
